name: turnoutChange
tooltip: Expected change in voter turnout compared to the previous election. Positive values indicate higher mobilization, which often benefits opposition parties.

name: incumbencyFatigue
tooltip: Additional penalty applied to incumbent candidates beyond the baseline anti-incumbency factor. Higher values reflect stronger voter desire for change.

name: turncoatPenalty
tooltip: Vote share penalty for candidates who switched parties since the last election. Applied only to candidates flagged as turncoats in the TCPD data.

name: recontestBonus
tooltip: Vote share bonus for candidates re-contesting from the same constituency. Reflects the advantage of name recognition and local network.

name: sameConstituencyBonus
tooltip: Bonus for candidates contesting from the same constituency as their previous election, reflecting local familiarity advantage.

name: previousMarginFactor
tooltip: How much the previous election's margin of victory influences the next result. Positive values strengthen leads, negative values indicate mean-reversion (close races tend to stay close).

name: enopFactor
tooltip: Effect of the Effective Number of Parties (ENOP). Higher ENOP means more fragmented competition, typically reducing the winner's vote share.

name: nCandFactor
tooltip: Effect of the number of candidates contesting. More candidates typically fragment votes and reduce the winner's margin.

name: constituencyTypeFactor
tooltip: Differential effect for SC/ST reserved constituencies. Positive values boost ruling party performance in reserved seats.

name: genderFactor
tooltip: Expected vote share adjustment for female candidates. Positive values indicate a gender advantage; negative values indicate a penalty.

name: partyStrengthFactor
tooltip: Weight given to a party's historical strength (seat count and average vote share) in the state. Positive values entrench dominant parties.

name: partyVoteShareFactor
tooltip: How much a party's average vote share trend from the previous election influences individual constituency predictions. Captures party-level momentum.
